ptxt.intxrefpoff: Windshield Wiper Motor Removal and Installation

s1.ptxt: Make marks (1) as shown in figure with windshield wiper motor stopped.

Connect battery positive (+) and negative (–) terminals to windshield wiper motor connector terminals as shown to operate windshield motor at low speed. If windshield wiper motor does not operate, replace windshield wiper motor.

Connect battery positive (+) and negative (–) terminals to windshield wiper motor connector terminals as shown to operate windshield motor at high speed. If windshield wiper motor does not operate, replace windshield wiper motor.
Perform the following steps to check that windshield wiper motor stops at wiper park position.

s2.ptxt: Connect lead from battery positive (+) terminal to terminal “1” of windshield wiper motor connector. Connect lead from battery negative (–) terminal to terminal “5” of windshield wiper motor connector. Windshield wiper motor will start rotating.
Disconnect lead from terminal “1” of windshield wiper motor connector to stop windshield wiper motor when marks (1) are not aligned.

Connect jumper wire between terminals “3” and “1” of windshield wiper motor connector. Then connect lead from battery positive (+) terminal to terminal “2” of windshield wiper motor connector and connect lead from battery negative (–) terminal to terminal “5” of windshield wiper motor connector. Check that windshield wiper motor starts rotating again and then stops when marks (1) are aligned.
Repeat Steps 1) through 3) several times to check that the windshield motor stops every time when marks are aligned.
If check result is not satisfactory, replace windshield wiper motor.
